message:
style: rustfmt formatting in bench_schema, perf_baseline_variance, and session_store_v2 tests

Apply rustfmt canonical formatting across three test files:

- bench_schema.rs: Wrap long array literals with one element per line for
  stage_attribution field validation; use Rust numeric literal separators
  (100_000 instead of 100000) in phase1 matrix golden fixture and rejection
  test JSON values; reformat long assert_eq! with multi-line alignment
- perf_baseline_variance.rs: Reorder serde_json imports to alphabetical
  (Value before json → json after Value per rustfmt convention)
- session_store_v2.rs: Reflow long fs::write() calls, recover_partial_migration
  bindings, format string literals, and assert!() messages to comply with
  default rustfmt line-width limits; affects e2e_partial_migration_recovery,
  e2e_corrupt_migration_recovery, e2e_migration_state_machine_transitions,
  e2e_large_session_migration_integrity, e2e_recover_unmigrated_is_noop,
  e2e_recover_migrated_is_noop, e2e_forensic_ledger, e2e_rapid_migrate_rollback,
  and e2e_verification_detects_post_migration tests

No logic changes — purely mechanical formatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
